content:
# Hypersaline

**Summary**: Water with a salt concentration significantly higher than average seawater.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] studied foraminifera adapted to hypersaline conditions in the Arabian Gulf.

- [[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023]] investigated communities in the hypersaline environment of the western Arabian Gulf.

## Scientific Context

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] Determines the types of organisms that can survive in the region.

- [[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023]] investigated communities in the hypersaline environment of the western Arabian Gulf.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] Measured via refractometer.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Gulf is one of the most extensive stretches of hypersaline shelf on earth. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 134]])


- Salinity levels in Murray's Pool ranged from 43 to 47.5 PSU, while others reached over 60 PSU. ([[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023, p. 36]])
